commands.review_summary:
This PR adds a QA Lab channel behavior scenario driver, exports runtime helpers/types, adds `conversation` and `reset` flow actions, and converts `dm-chat-baseline` to the new conversation action.

PR surface: Source +792, Tests +857, Other +1. Total +1650 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR adds QA Lab authoring infrastructure rather than fixing a reproduced runtime bug. Current main and the PR head show the behavior difference clearly.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is appropriate because this protected maintainer-labeled PR expands the QA Lab runtime/YAML contract and has no narrow automated repair finding.

target.title: test(qa): route DM baseline through channel scenario driver
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The QA real-E2E migration plan needs channel scenarios to be routed through a reusable behavior contract instead of hand-wiring low-level `state.addInboundMessage` / `waitForOutboundMessage` calls in every YAML file. Without that shared path, live, Crabline, and qa-channel variants can drift and weak scenarios can look covered while skipping thread/reply/no-reply semantics.

## Why This Change Was Made

Adds the QA Lab channel behavior scenario module with typed scenario definitions, expected reply/no-reply steps, restart/thread/reply requirement summaries, target/message helpers, and a `ChannelScenarioDriver` interface.

This also wires one concrete existing scenario through that path: `qa/scenarios/channels/dm-chat-baseline.yaml` now uses a native `conversation:` flow action, so the YAML defines target, sender, turns, and expectations while the runtime API owns the QA-channel driver plumbing.

The runner deliberately fails fast for reply-targeting steps until those semantics are implemented, and created-thread reply expectations are pinned to the dynamic thread id to avoid false-positive threaded evidence.

AI-assisted: yes.

## User Impact

No end-user runtime behavior change. QA authors now have a shared contract and a working concise example for routing channel behavior through a pluggable driver, with `dm-chat-baseline` proving the path against the existing qa-channel suite.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/channel-behavior-scenario.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/scenario-flow-runner.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/scenario-runtime-api.test.ts --reporter=verbose` pass, 23 tests.
- `pnpm openclaw qa suite --provider-mode mock-openai --scenario dm-chat-baseline --output-dir .artifacts/qa-e2e/dm-channel-runtime-helper-smoke` pass, 1 scenario passed / 0 failed.
- `pnpm openclaw qa suite --provider-mode mock-openai --scenario dm-chat-baseline --output-dir .artifacts/qa-e2e/dm-channel-native-yaml-smoke` pass after converting the scenario definition to native YAML.
- `pnpm openclaw qa suite --provider-mode mock-openai --scenario dm-chat-baseline --output-dir .artifacts/qa-e2e/dm-channel-conversation-action-cyclefix-smoke` pass after adding the native `conversation` flow action and cycle fix.
- `pnpm check:architecture` pass after moving QA transport bus types to the leaf protocol module.
- `pnpm check:test-types` pass.
- `pnpm deadcode:unused-files` pass.
- `pnpm deadcode:dependencies` pass.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/qa-lab/src/channel-behavior-scenario.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/channel-behavior-scenario.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/scenario-runtime-api.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/scenario-runtime-api.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/runtime-api.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/scenario-flow-runner.test.ts qa/scenarios/channels/dm-chat-baseline.yaml` pass.
- `git diff --check` pass.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` clean after simplification.
